senses.definition: A decline or decrease in size, amount, or degree; a reduction.
examples: There was a recrease in profits this quarter.
The recrease in population affected local services.

senses.definition: Capable of being created again or brought back into existence.
examples: The data is recreatable from backups.
The scene is recreatable with the right software.

senses.definition: To create again; to make something exist or appear again.
examples: We recreated the old mural for the exhibit.
The team tried to recreate the experiment.
Fans came to recreate the scene from the movie.

senses.definition: Past tense of recreate; was created again or produced again.
examples: The sculpture was recreated after the original was damaged.
She recreated the melody from memory.

senses.definition: Third person singular present of recreate; creates again or provides recreation.
examples: The park recreates a sense of nostalgia for visitors.
She recreates the scene with impressive accuracy.

senses.definition: Present participle of recreate; engaging in activities for enjoyment or to restore energy.
examples: People are recreating in the park on weekends.
He spends the afternoons recreating with old friends.

senses.definition: Activities done for enjoyment when you are not working.
examples: Reading is my favorite recreation.
Sports provide recreation after a long day.

senses.definition: Relating to or used for recreation or leisure.
examples: They enjoyed recreational activities at the resort.
Recreational drugs are illegal in many countries.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to recreation or leisure; in a recreational manner.
examples: The town is recreationally oriented, with many parks.
They spend time recreationally rather than work-related activities.

senses.definition: A person who studies or advocates for recreation as a concept or practice.
examples: The recreationist spoke about community leisure programs.
Recreationists organized a weekend outdoor event.

senses.definition: Plural of recreationist; people who study or advocate for recreation.
examples: The conference gathered several recreationists from different cities.
Recreationists discussed best practices for parks.

senses.definition: Things created again; also things made for entertainment that imitate real life.
examples: The old theater shows nostalgic recreations of dances.
The game offers several recreations of famous scenes.

senses.definition: Related to or suitable for recreation; enjoyable and relaxing.
examples: A recreative walk in the park improved her mood.
The hotel offers recreative activities and facilities.

senses.definition: In a manner that is related to recreation or creative leisure.
examples: They engaged recreatively, drawing and storytelling.
The studio uses space recreatively to encourage ideas.

senses.definition: The quality of being recreational or creating leisure and amusement.
examples: The city prized its re-creative recreativeness in parks.
Re-creativeness can boost community engagement.

senses.definition: A person who recreates or creates for recreation; someone who engages in leisure activities.
examples: The recreator joined the outdoor club.
She is a passionate re creator of vintage games.

senses.definition: Plural of recreator; people who recreate or create for recreation.
examples: The workshop gathered several recreators from the community.
Recreators shared tips for creative leisure activities.

senses.definition: Of or relating to recreation or to places intended for leisure.
examples: The hotel offers recreational spaces and a recreatory lounge.
Recreatory facilities include gyms and parks.

senses.definition: To credential again; to provide new credentials or verify qualifications anew.
examples: The organization decided to recredential the staff after a policy change.
They will recredential doctors to ensure up-to-date licenses.

senses.definition: To credit again; to add funds or recognition again to an account or record.
examples: They will recredit the account after the error is found.
The company moved to recredit customer points.

senses.definition: Historical or rare term referring to a thing that is coming down or decreasing; in some contexts relating to digestion as a bodily excretion.
examples: In archaic medical texts, recrement is noted as waste.
The term recrement appears in older scholarly works.

senses.definition: Related to recrement; pertaining to excretory waste, in historical or technical contexts.
examples: The rare, recremental term appeared in the old anatomist's notes.

senses.definition: Variant form relating to recrement; used in highly specialized historical texts.
examples: The manuscript uses a recrementitial form to describe waste.

senses.definition: Of or relating to recrement; containing or producing waste.
examples: The recrementitious process was documented in the 19th-century journal.

senses.definition: Plural of recrement; substances excreted as waste in older or technical language.
examples: The text mentions several recrements.
Medical readers would note the term recrements.
